description: OpenClaw Video Format Library — 10 distinct repeatable short-form video formats for variety across platforms.

formats.name: Presenter + Product UI Walkthrough
formats.ideal_use_case: Demonstrating a specific product feature or workflow by showing the real UI alongside the presenter. Best for product launches, feature announcements, and how-to content.

scene_structure.description: Avatar faces camera, delivers a one-line hook about the feature or problem it solves.
Screen splits: presenter on the left third, product UI screenshot or recording on the right two-thirds. Presenter narrates as UI highlights animate.
UI takes full screen with zoomed callouts on key interactions. Voiceover continues.
Presenter returns full-frame, recaps the value delivered by the feature.
Branded endcard with URL, QR code, or call-to-action text overlay.

formats.screenshot_usage: Split-screen and fullscreen scenes use real product screenshots or screen recordings. Screenshots must be current and match the narration exactly.
formats.variety_notes: Vary which product and feature you showcase each time. Alternate between split-screen-left and split-screen-right layouts. Rotate hook styles: question, stat, or bold claim.

formats.name: Presenter with Feature Pop-Ins
formats.ideal_use_case: Highlighting 3-5 key features or benefits in rapid succession. Great for product overviews, comparison posts, and listicle-style content.

scene_structure.description: Avatar opens with an attention-grabbing question or statement about the product category.
Presenter continues speaking. A branded card or icon pops in from the side showing feature name and one-line benefit.
Previous card exits, next feature card pops in from opposite side. Presenter gestures or shifts gaze.
Third feature card pops in. Optionally include a mini screenshot thumbnail inside the card.
All three feature cards stack or tile on screen briefly, then exit. Presenter delivers closing line.
Lower-third CTA bar with link and brand logo while presenter stays on screen.

formats.screenshot_usage: Optional small screenshot thumbnails inside feature pop-in cards. Not required if the feature can be described with an icon and text.
formats.variety_notes: Vary the number of pop-ins (3 to 5). Alternate pop-in directions (left, right, top). Use different card color schemes per product. Occasionally replace text cards with animated icons.

formats.name: Presenter with Proof/Result Overlays
formats.ideal_use_case: Showcasing traction, results, metrics, or social proof. Ideal for milestone announcements, user testimonials, and credibility-building content.

scene_structure.description: Avatar opens with a bold result statement or question: 'Here is what happened when we...'
Large animated number or stat overlays beside the presenter. Counter animation or reveal effect.
Real screenshot evidence (dashboard, tweet, review, GitHub stars) appears as a floating overlay. Presenter narrates context.
A user quote or testimonial text overlays on a branded background card beside the presenter.
Overlays clear. Presenter delivers the takeaway message.
Endcard with CTA.

formats.screenshot_usage: Real screenshots of dashboards, analytics, tweets, reviews, or GitHub activity used as proof overlays. Must be genuine and current.
formats.variety_notes: Rotate proof types: metrics one week, user quotes the next, GitHub activity after that. Vary overlay animation styles (slide-in, fade, zoom). Never repeat the same stat in consecutive videos.

formats.name: Problem/Solution with Presenter
formats.ideal_use_case: Addressing a specific pain point and showing how the product solves it. Best for awareness-stage content, pain-point marketing, and objection handling.

scene_structure.description: Presenter describes the problem with urgency. Background shifts to a muted or red-tinted tone to signal frustration.
Text overlays or icons amplify the problem: stats about the issue, common complaints, or relatable scenarios.
Visual transition: background shifts to bright/blue tone. Presenter says 'But here is the thing...' or similar pivot.
Presenter introduces the product as the solution. Product screenshot or logo appears alongside.
Quick proof point: screenshot, stat, or user quote reinforcing the solution works.
Endcard with URL and call-to-action.

formats.screenshot_usage: Product screenshot appears during solution-reveal scene. Optional proof screenshot in solution-proof scene.
formats.variety_notes: Rotate problems from different angles: cost, time, complexity, accessibility. Vary the transition style between problem and solution. Alternate between stat-based and story-based problem amplification.

formats.name: Screenshot-Led Explainer with Presenter
formats.ideal_use_case: Deep-dive walkthrough of a specific screen, page, or workflow. Best for tutorials, onboarding content, and detailed feature education.

scene_structure.description: Presenter introduces what the viewer will learn. Clean background.
Full-screen product screenshot with animated highlight boxes and arrows pointing to key areas. Voiceover explains step by step.
Second screenshot showing the next step or result. Zoom-in on important details.
Picture-in-picture: small presenter circle in corner, screenshot fills rest of frame. Presenter adds context.
Presenter returns full-frame for a quick recap of what was covered.
Endcard with link to try the product or read documentation.

formats.screenshot_usage: Primary content driver. Multiple real product screenshots with animated annotations (arrows, highlights, zoom). Screenshots must be high-resolution and current.
formats.variety_notes: Cover different products and screens each time. Alternate between 2-step and 3-step walkthroughs. Vary annotation styles: arrows one time, highlight boxes the next, numbered circles after that.

formats.name: Mission/Vision Short with Presenter
formats.ideal_use_case: Founder-voice content about why the company exists, its values, or its long-term vision. Best for brand building, hiring, and community engagement.

scene_structure.description: Presenter opens with a personal or philosophical statement. Warm, conversational tone. Minimal background.
Key mission phrase appears as large text beside or behind the presenter. Typography-driven visual.
Presenter tells a brief story or shares a specific moment that shaped the mission. Optional B-roll-style background images.
Presenter states the mission clearly and directly. Text overlay reinforces the core statement.
Brand logo, tagline, and optional community link. No hard-sell CTA.

formats.screenshot_usage: None or minimal. This format is personality-driven, not product-driven. Optional use of product logos or website hero screenshots as subtle background elements.
formats.variety_notes: Rotate between mission, vision, values, and origin-story angles. Vary background styles: solid color, gradient, subtle texture. Never repeat the same core statement in consecutive videos.

formats.name: Product-First Reel with Presenter Bookends
formats.ideal_use_case: Leading with the product experience rather than the presenter. Best for visually impressive features, UI showcases, and demo-style content where the product speaks for itself.

scene_structure.description: Brief presenter intro: 'Watch this' or 'Check out what X can do.' Sets context in one sentence.
Full-screen product UI or screen recording. No presenter visible. Voiceover optional. Music-driven pacing.
Second product view: different screen, different angle, or result of the action shown in showcase-1.
Third product view or final result. Quick zoom on the outcome.
Presenter returns: 'That is [product]. Link below.' Quick, punchy outro.
Endcard with link.

formats.screenshot_usage: Primary content. Three or more full-screen product screenshots or screen recordings fill the middle of the video. These must be the hero visuals.
formats.variety_notes: Vary the product shown. Alternate between 2 and 4 product showcase scenes. Try different music tempos. Occasionally use a slow zoom instead of cuts between screenshots.

formats.name: Community / Devlog Insider Update
formats.ideal_use_case: Raw, casual development updates for the community. No avatar needed. Best for devlog-style updates, behind-the-scenes, changelog content, and community engagement.

scene_structure.description: Bold text card on branded background: 'Dev Update' or 'What we shipped this week'. No presenter.
Screenshot of the update: code diff, new UI, dashboard metric, or GitHub commit. Text overlay labels what changed.
Second update screenshot. Different product or different aspect of the same update.
Third update or community highlight: Discord message, user feedback, or upcoming roadmap item.
Closing card: 'Join the Discord' or 'Star us on GitHub' with link. Community-focused CTA.

formats.screenshot_usage: Core content. Real screenshots of code changes, UI updates, GitHub activity, Discord conversations, or analytics dashboards. Raw and authentic feel.
formats.variety_notes: Vary between single-product deep updates and multi-product round-ups. Alternate color schemes. Rotate between 2 and 4 update items. Use different music tracks for energy variation.

formats.name: LinkedIn Authority Clip
formats.ideal_use_case: Thought leadership and professional credibility content. Presenter delivers a sharp insight, opinion, or lesson. Best for LinkedIn native video, founder branding, and industry commentary.

scene_structure.description: Presenter opens with a bold, slightly contrarian statement or hard-won lesson. Direct eye contact. Clean professional background.
Key insight appears as a clean text card beside the presenter. Professional typography. One sentence maximum.
Presenter elaborates with a specific example, data point, or experience. Optional subtle data visualization overlay.
Takeaway statement appears as a second text card. Presenter reinforces it verbally.
Presenter closes by asking the audience a question to drive comments. Lower-third shows name and title.
Subtle brand bar at bottom: logo, website. No hard CTA.

formats.screenshot_usage: Minimal. Optional use of a data chart, industry report screenshot, or product screenshot as supporting evidence during elaboration. Never the primary visual.
formats.variety_notes: Rotate topics across: AI industry insights, founder lessons, India tech ecosystem, building in public. Vary text card styles: white-on-dark, dark-on-light, accent-colored. Alternate between question-close and statement-close.

formats.name: X Teaser / Ultra-Short Cutdown
formats.ideal_use_case: Ultra-short teaser content for X (Twitter). Maximum impact in minimum time. Best for announcements, hype-building, feature teases, and driving traffic to longer content.

scene_structure.description: Bold text on branded background flashes the hook. One line maximum. Fast-paced music or sound effect.
Quick product screenshot or key visual. Minimal or no narration. Text overlay states the one thing the viewer needs to know.
CTA text: 'Try it now', 'Link in bio', or 'Full video on YouTube'. Brand logo. Fast exit.

formats.screenshot_usage: One or two maximum-impact product screenshots. The screenshot must instantly communicate value with no explanation needed.
formats.variety_notes: Vary text colors and background patterns. Alternate between product screenshots and typographic-only teasers. Use different sound effects. Rotate between announcement, question, and stat hook styles.
